title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal direct risk, but improper use or reliance on it without validation could lead to missed vulnerabilities or false positives. Additionally, if the tool is compromised or contains malicious code, it could introduce security risks.
recommended_actions: Conduct a thorough review of the tool's source code and functionality before integrating it into workflows.
Use the tool as a supplementary measure alongside established vulnerability scanning solutions.
Monitor the tool's updates and community feedback to ensure it remains secure and reliable.

title: The WP Job Portal plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file deletion due to...
description: The WP Job Portal plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to arbitrary file deletion due to insufficient file path validation in the 'WPJOBPORTALcustomfields::removeFileCustom' function in all versions up to, and including, 2.4.9. This makes it possible for authenticated attackers, with Subscriber-level access and above, to delete arbitrary files on the server, which can easily lead to remote code execution when the right file is deleted (such as wp-config.php).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows authenticated attackers with minimal privileges (Subscriber-level access) to delete arbitrary files on the server. This can lead to critical issues such as remote code execution, denial of service, or complete compromise of the WordPress site if sensitive files like wp-config.php are deleted.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the WP Job Portal plugin to a patched version (if available) or disable it until a fix is released.
Restrict access to accounts with Subscriber-level privileges and audit user roles to minimize exposure.
Implement a web application firewall (WAF) to monitor and block malicious activity targeting this vulnerability.

This vulnerability affects unknown code of the file /update_stock.php of the component HTTP GET Parameter Handler. This manipulation of the argument sid causes sql injection. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. If you want to get best quality of vulnerability data, you may have to visit VulDB.

title: A flaw has been found in Enter Software Iperius Backup up to 8.7.3. Affected by this...
description: A flaw has been found in Enter Software Iperius Backup up to 8.7.3. Affected by this vulnerability is an unknown functionality of the component NTLM2 Handler. Executing a manipulation can lead to information disclosure. The attack is restricted to local execution. Attacks of this nature are highly complex. The exploitation appears to be difficult. The exploit has been published and may be used. Upgrading to version 8.7.4 addresses this issue. Upgrading the affected component is advised. The vend

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the NTLM2 Handler of Iperius Backup up to version 8.7.3 can lead to information disclosure. However, the attack requires local execution, is highly complex, and difficult to exploit. The impact is limited due to these constraints.
recommended_actions: Upgrade Iperius Backup to version 8.7.4 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict local access to trusted personnel to reduce the risk of exploitation.
Conduct a review of backup configurations and ensure sensitive data is encrypted to minimize potential exposure.

title: A vulnerability has been found in Enter Software Iperius Backup up to 8.7.3. Affected by this...
description: A vulnerability has been found in Enter Software Iperius Backup up to 8.7.3. Affected by this issue is some unknown functionality of the component Backup Job Configuration File Handler. The manipulation leads to improper privilege management. The attack must be carried out locally. The attack is considered to have high complexity. The exploitation is known to be difficult. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. Upgrading to version 8.7.4 can resolve this issue. It is advis

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Iperius Backup up to version 8.7.3 allows improper privilege management through the Backup Job Configuration File Handler. If exploited, it could enable unauthorized access or privilege escalation, potentially compromising sensitive backup data. However, the attack requires local access and is of high complexity, reducing its likelihood of exploitation.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Iperius Backup version 8.7.4 or later to mitigate the vulnerability.
Restrict physical and local access to systems running Iperius Backup to trusted personnel only.
Conduct regular security audits and monitor for any unauthorized access attempts or unusual activity on systems using Iperius Backup.

title: n8n's Source Control SSH Configuration Uses StrictHostKeyChecking=no
description:
## Impact
When the Source Control feature is configured to use SSH, the SSH command used for git operations explicitly disabled host key verification. A network attacker positioned between the n8n instance and the remote Git server could intercept the connection and present a fraudulent host key, potentially injecting malicious content into workflows or intercepting repository data.

- This issue only affects instances where the Source Control feature has been explicitly enabled and configured t

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33724.

title: n8n Has External Secrets Authorization Bypass in Credential Saving
description:
## Impact
An authenticated user without permission to list external secrets could reference a secret by the external name in a credential and retrieve its plaintext value when saving the credential. This bypassed the `externalSecret:list` permission check and allowed access to secrets stored in connected vaults without admin or owner privileges.

- This issue requires the instance to have an external secrets vault configured.
- The attacker must know or be able to guess the name of a target secr

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33722.

title: Saloon has a Fixture Name Path Traversal Vulnerability
description:
### Impact
Users with MockResponse fixtures that use path traversal.

### Patches
Upgrade to Saloon v4+

Upgrade guide: https://docs.saloon.dev/upgrade/upgrading-from-v3-to-v4

### Description
Fixture names were used to build file paths under the configured fixture directory without validation. A name containing path segments (e.g. ../traversal or ../../etc/passwd) resulted in a path outside that directory. When the application read a fixture (e.g. for mocking) or wrote one (e.g. when recording

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to perform path traversal attacks, potentially accessing or modifying sensitive files outside the intended directory. This could lead to data breaches, unauthorized access, or manipulation of application behavior.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to Saloon v4 or higher as per the provided upgrade guide to mitigate the vulnerability.
Conduct a thorough review of applications using Saloon v3 to identify any potential misuse of fixture names and ensure no sensitive data has been exposed.
Implement additional input validation and sanitization for fixture names to prevent path traversal attacks in the future.

title: Saloon is vulnerable to SSRF and credential leakage via absolute URL in endpoint overriding base URL
description:
### Impact
Users providing user generated input into the `resolveEndpoint` method on requests.

### Patches
Upgrade to Saloon v4+

Upgrade guide: https://docs.saloon.dev/upgrade/upgrading-from-v3-to-v4

### Description
When building the request URL, Saloon combined the connector's base URL with the request endpoint. If the endpoint was a valid absolute URL (e.g. https://attacker.example.com/callback), the code used that URL as-is and ignored the base URL. The request—and any authentication heade

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33182.

title: n8n Has Authorization Bypass in OAuth Callback via N8N_SKIP_AUTH_ON_OAUTH_CALLBACK
description:
## Impact
When the `N8N_SKIP_AUTH_ON_OAUTH_CALLBACK` environment variable is set to `true`, the OAuth callback handler skips ownership verification of the OAuth state parameter. This allows an attacker to trick a victim into completing an OAuth flow against a credential object the attacker controls, causing the victim's OAuth tokens to be stored in the attacker's credential. The attacker can then use those tokens to execute workflows in their name.

- This issue only affects instances where `N8N

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows an attacker to hijack OAuth tokens from victims, enabling unauthorized execution of workflows in the victim's name. This could lead to data breaches, unauthorized actions, and potential reputational damage.
recommended_actions: Disable the N8N_SKIP_AUTH_ON_OAUTH_CALLBACK environment variable or set it to false to ensure proper ownership verification during OAuth callbacks.
Update to the latest patched version of n8n as soon as it becomes available to mitigate the vulnerability.
Review and monitor OAuth configurations and credential objects for any suspicious activity or unauthorized access.

title: AVideo is Vulnerable to SQL Injection through Subscribe Endpoint via Unsanitized user_id Parameter
description:
## Summary

The `Subscribe::save()` method in `objects/subscribe.php` concatenates the `$this->users_id` property directly into an INSERT SQL query without sanitization or parameterized binding. This property originates from `$_POST['user_id']` in both `subscribe.json.php` and `subscribeNotify.json.php`. An authenticated attacker can inject arbitrary SQL to extract sensitive data from any database table, including password hashes, API keys, and encryption salts.

## Details

The vulnerability ex

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The SQL injection vulnerability in AVideo's Subscribe endpoint allows authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary SQL queries, potentially exposing sensitive data such as password hashes, API keys, and encryption salts. This could lead to data breaches, unauthorized access, and system compromise.
recommended_actions: Implement parameterized queries and input validation for the user_id parameter in the Subscribe::save() method.
Perform a comprehensive code audit to identify and remediate similar vulnerabilities across the application.
Update to the latest patched version of AVideo or apply security patches provided by the vendor.

title: AVideo: Unauthenticated CDN Configuration Takeover via Empty Default Key Bypass and Mass-Assignment
description:
## Summary

The CDN plugin endpoints `plugin/CDN/status.json.php` and `plugin/CDN/disable.json.php` use key-based authentication with an empty string default key. When the CDN plugin is enabled but the key has not been configured (the default state), the key validation check is completely bypassed, allowing any unauthenticated attacker to modify the full CDN configuration — including CDN URLs, storage credentials, and the authentication key itself — via mass-assignment through the `par` request

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: This vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to take over the CDN configuration of AVideo instances, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive storage credentials, manipulation of CDN URLs, and disruption of content delivery services. This could result in data breaches, service outages, and reputational damage.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the AVideo CDN plugin to the latest patched version that addresses CVE-2026-33719.
Implement strict authentication mechanisms for all CDN-related endpoints, ensuring that default keys are removed or securely configured.
Conduct a thorough audit of CDN configurations to identify and remediate any unauthorized changes.

title: OpenHands is Vulnerable to Command Injection through its Git Diff Handler
description:
## Summary

A Command Injection vulnerability exists in the `get_git_diff()` method at `openhands/runtime/utils/git_handler.py:134`. The `path` parameter from the `/api/conversations/{conversation_id}/git/diff` API endpoint is passed unsanitized to a shell command, allowing authenticated attackers to execute arbitrary commands in the agent sandbox. The user is already allowed to instruct the agent to execute commands, but this bypasses the normal channels.

---

### Vulnerable Code P

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-33718.

title: CVE-2026-31993 - OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.22 contain an allowlist parsing mismatch vulnerability in the macO
description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.22 contain an allowlist parsing mismatch vulnerability in the macOS companion app that allows authenticated operators to bypass exec approval checks. Attackers with operator.write privileges and a paired macOS beta node can craft shell-chain payloads that pass incomplete allowlist validation and execute arbitrary commands on the paired host.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-31993.

title: CVE-2026-31992 - OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.23 contain an allowlist bypass vulnerability in system.run guardra
description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.23 contain an allowlist bypass vulnerability in system.run guardrails that allows authenticated operators to execute unintended commands. When /usr/bin/env is allowlisted, attackers can use env -S to bypass policy analysis and execute shell wrapper payloads at runtime.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-31992.

title: CVE-2026-31991 - OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.26 contain an authorization bypass vulnerability where Signal grou
description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.26 contain an authorization bypass vulnerability where Signal group allowlist policy incorrectly accepts sender identities from DM pairing-store approvals. Attackers can exploit this boundary weakness by obtaining DM pairing approval to bypass group allowlist checks and gain unauthorized group access.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity. CVE: CVE-2026-31991.

title: CVE-2026-31990 - OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.3.2 contain a vulnerability in the stageSandboxMedia function in whi
description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.3.2 contain a vulnerability in the stageSandboxMedia function in which it fails to validate destination symlinks during media staging, allowing writes to follow symlinks outside the sandbox workspace. Attackers can exploit this by placing symlinks in the media/inbound directory to overwrite arbitrary files on the host system outside sandbox boundaries.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-31990.

title: CVE-2026-31989 - OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.3.1 contain a server-side request forgery vulnerability in web_searc
description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.3.1 contain a server-side request forgery vulnerability in web_search citation redirect resolution that uses a private-network-allowing SSRF policy. An attacker who can influence citation redirect targets can trigger internal-network requests from the OpenClaw host to loopback, private, or internal destinations.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-31989.

title: CVE-2026-29608 - OpenClaw 2026.3.1 contains an approval integrity vulnerability in system.run node-host execution whe
description: OpenClaw 2026.3.1 contains an approval integrity vulnerability in system.run node-host execution where argv rewriting changes command semantics. Attackers can place malicious local scripts in the working directory to execute unintended code despite operator approval of different command text.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-29608.

title: CVE-2026-29607 - OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.22 contain an authorization bypass vulnerability in allow-always w
description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.22 contain an authorization bypass vulnerability in allow-always wrapper persistence that allows attackers to bypass approval checks by persisting wrapper-level allowlist entries instead of validating inner executable intent. Remote attackers can approve benign wrapped system.run commands and subsequently execute different payloads without approval, enabling remote code execution on gateway and node-host execution flows.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-29607.

title: CVE-2026-28461 - OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.3.1 contain an unbounded memory growth vulnerability in the Zalo web
description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.3.1 contain an unbounded memory growth vulnerability in the Zalo webhook endpoint that allows unauthenticated attackers to trigger in-memory key accumulation by varying query strings. Remote attackers can exploit this by sending repeated requests with different query parameters to cause memory pressure, process instability, or out-of-memory conditions that degrade service availability.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28461.

title: CVE-2026-28460 - OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.22 contain an allowlist bypass vulnerability in system.run that al
description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.22 contain an allowlist bypass vulnerability in system.run that allows attackers to execute non-allowlisted commands by splitting command substitution using shell line-continuation characters. Attackers can bypass security analysis by injecting $\\ followed by a newline and opening parenthesis inside double quotes, causing the shell to fold the line continuation into executable command substitution that circumvents approval boundaries.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28460.

title: CVE-2026-28449 - OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.25 lack durable replay state for Nextcloud Talk webhook events, al
description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.25 lack durable replay state for Nextcloud Talk webhook events, allowing valid signed webhook requests to be replayed without suppression. Attackers can capture and replay previously valid signed webhook requests to trigger duplicate inbound message processing and cause integrity or availability issues.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-28449.

title: CVE-2026-27670 - OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.3.2 contain a race condition vulnerability in ZIP extraction that al
description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.3.2 contain a race condition vulnerability in ZIP extraction that allows local attackers to write files outside the intended destination directory. Attackers can exploit a time-of-check-time-of-use race between path validation and file write operations by rebinding parent directory symlinks to redirect writes outside the extraction root.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-27670.

title: CVE-2026-27566 - OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.22 contain an allowlist bypass vulnerability in system.run exec an
description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.22 contain an allowlist bypass vulnerability in system.run exec analysis that fails to unwrap env and shell-dispatch wrapper chains. Attackers can route execution through wrapper binaries like env bash to smuggle payloads that satisfy allowlist entries while executing non-allowlisted commands.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-27566.

title: CVE-2026-22176 - OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.19 contain a command injection vulnerability in Windows Scheduled
description: OpenClaw versions prior to 2026.2.19 contain a command injection vulnerability in Windows Scheduled Task script generation where environment variables are written to gateway.cmd using unquoted set KEY=VALUE assignments, allowing shell metacharacters to break out of assignment context. Attackers can inject arbitrary commands through environment variable values containing metacharacters like &, |, ^, %, or ! to achieve command execution when the scheduled task script is generated and executed.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-22176.

title: CVE-2026-32743 - PX4 is an open-source autopilot stack for drones and unmanned vehicles. Versions 1.17.0-rc2 and belo
description: PX4 is an open-source autopilot stack for drones and unmanned vehicles. Versions 1.17.0-rc2 and below are vulnerable to Stack-based Buffer Overflow through the MavlinkLogHandler, and are triggered via MAVLink log request. The LogEntry.filepath buffer is 60 bytes, but the sscanf function parses paths from the log list file with no width specifier, allowing a path longer than 60 characters to overflow the buffer. An attacker with MAVLink link access can trigger this by first creating deeply nested directories via MAVLink FTP, then requesting the log list. The flight controller MAVLink task crashes, losing telemetry and command capability and causing DoS. This issue has been fixed in this commit: https://github.com/PX4/PX4-Autopilot/commit/616b25a280e229c24d5cf12a03dbf248df89c474.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-32743.

title: CVE-2026-32255 - Kan is an open-source project management tool. In versions 0.5.4 and below, the /api/download/attatc
description: Kan is an open-source project management tool. In versions 0.5.4 and below, the /api/download/attatchment endpoint has no authentication and no URL validation. The Attachment Download endpoint accepts a user-supplied URL query parameter and passes it directly to fetch() server-side, and returns the full response body. An unauthenticated attacker can use this to make HTTP requests from the server to internal services, cloud metadata endpoints, or private network resources. This issue has been fixed in version 0.5.5. To workaround this issue, block or restrict access to /api/download/attatchment at the reverse proxy level (nginx, Cloudflare, etc.).

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-32255.
